answers.assignedRole: BID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.difficultAspects: My medication bottle broke. This MedAdhere system also was dysfunctional

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.difficultAspects: The website went down, so many of the data points were not logged.

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.difficultAspects: I don't believe it can be changed, but I found it was often easy to take the medication, but a hassle to have to be near my computer to record that I took it.

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Reminders from CHWs.
answers.difficultAspects: The medadhere site went down for 2 days.

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: The bottles are cool
answers.difficultAspects: The recording site was broken half the time

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a TID patient
answers.beneficialAspects: The ease of using online applications for medication adherence.
answers.difficultAspects: Some technical issues with logging patient reminders
chwBarriers: Didn't want to bother my patient

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: I like that we were put into the patient's shoes, and it's nice to be on the other side of the curtain to know what they experience
answers.difficultAspects: My only issue was the software would go down when I needed to log my meds. I never missed a dose but there were times I couldn't log the dose I took. I also wish there was a way to delete a false response because I clicked it late at night once to see if it started working again and it got logged without me being able to remove the response.
answers.otherComments: I'm not sure how other people did, but I know personally I didn't miss any doses or get any help from the community health worker. That being said, this is a bit unrealistic because we're all medical students who have a lot of free time during transitions and are motivated because we know we're part of a study that is only temporary, so that is going to bias the outcome of any study that is done. Based on these differences, it's a valuable learning tool to learn empathy for patients, but the results are unlikely to be generalizable to any population.
answers.pillBottleFeedback: The pill bottle sensor would come off half the time I used it, so I had to put it back in, and I like the redundancy of having the system online to log my meds. I just think it needs to get its bugs fixed so we can log all our doses.

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Having an online hub that allows easy access to log medication adherence.
answers.difficultAspects: Improved connectivity would help patients log things appropriately, even if they already took the medication.
answers.otherComments: Discussing with CHW’s about including links to the adherence website in their correspondence to patients could improve logging and accurate tracking of medication adherence.
answers.pillBottleFeedback: The pill bottles did not close as tightly as I was expecting them to, and I did not hear a “click” half the time that I closed it. I wasn’t sure if it was accurately tracking when I opened and closed the pill bottles thereafter.

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
TID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Easy to log in times for taking meds on this app
answers.difficultAspects: My CHW only reached out to me a couple times with reminders, and the timing was inconsistent so having a CHW ultimately didn’t help much.
answers.pillBottleFeedback: Was not sure if I was opening/closing it correctly for it to be tracked

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Showing the difficulty of maintaining even a TID schedule

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: It is a realistic simulation of being prescribed a medication. Having an extra thing to keep track of on top of all of the other current stresses was challenging but true to the patient experience.
answers.difficultAspects: As the patient, the role of the CHW was confusing. I wasn’t sure what information she had access to, so I had agreed with her to send a text when I took the meds so she’d know. It was hard to remember to take the medicine, log it on medadhere, and contact the CHW because inevitably something would come up between some of the steps. It would be helpful to have more guidance about what the role of the CHW is and, ideally, not have the patient be responsible for reaching out to the CHW. For example, if the CHW can log into medadhere to see whether the patient took it at a particular time instead of having the patient contact them I think it would more equally distribute the tasks.
answers.pillBottleFeedback: They’re difficult to open the first time and difficult to close.

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Limited time frame was appropriate for student buy in while still being long enough to genuinely simulate taking meds on a regular basis including over weekends when people are on less regular schedules
answers.difficultAspects: Instructions with the medadhere app, especially how many times per day you needed to record, was confusing. Also my cap broke
answers.pillBottleFeedback_noChw: Mine broke where part of the cap kept coming off so I was just doing the best I could to put it back on and was hoping it kept recording opening and closing correctly

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: For people who don't take daily meds, I think it is a good exercise.
answers.difficultAspects: Unfortunately, the website was down so I was not able to log all the times I took the medication and because I got out of the habit, I forgot to log this morning too although I took the medication. I also think that for BID, 10am is an early cutoff. I only missed one dose on Saturday and it was because I slept in on Saturday and missed the 10am cutoff but I usually can take my morning meds at 11am no problem.

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
BID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Learning what is like to have to take a scheduled medication daily.
answers.difficultAspects: Having to use both the medication bottle and the website was difficult at times with a busy schedule.

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient with an assigned community health worker
Community health worker for a BID patient
answers.beneficialAspects: I would keep pretty much everything the same. I liked being able to use different means to notify the patient, such as email or text.
answers.difficultAspects: I think the technological issues were the most frustrating. The website errors and not being able to log my notifications made it unclear whether they were logged.
chwStrategies: Discussion of strategies to help them remember

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a BID patient
Community health worker for a TID patient
answers.beneficialAspects: How difficult taking medications can be
answers.difficultAspects: Nothing really

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient with an assigned community health worker
Community health worker for a BID patient
chwBarriers: Didn't want to bother my patient

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a BID patient
answers.beneficialAspects: Good to remind myself how difficult patient medication management can be.
answers.difficultAspects: I think being post match and traveling a lot made this more difficult.

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a BID patient
answers.beneficialAspects: It required communication between classmates
answers.difficultAspects: Some people that aren’t interested in the class or project won’t remind their patient or open the pill bottle regardless if they have a community health worker or not.
answers.otherComments: Wish there wasn’t a network error for the few days that it happened.
chwBarriers: Didn't want to bother my patient

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: It helped medical students understand how difficult it can be for patients to take medications three or even two times a day.
answers.difficultAspects: The online reporting system did not work for 2-3 days, so I could not track that I was taking the medicine.
answers.pillBottleFeedback: Sometimes it was hard to open and I couldn't tell if I closed it correctly

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.difficultAspects: Software could be improved and need to give health worker and patient pairing earlier with contact info. I wasn’t contacted by the community health worker the entirety of the project
answers.pillBottleFeedback: The magnet kept coming off the bottle

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Med reminders were great!
answers.difficultAspects: Maybe more organized instructions and provide them earlier as well

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a TID patient
chwBarriers: Didn't want to bother my patient
chwStrategies: Discussion of strategies to help them remember

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Develops empathy
answers.difficultAspects: Network issues

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a BID patient
answers.beneficialAspects: Makes you appreciate the time and memory required for community healthcare workers to keep track of patients and their medications.
answers.difficultAspects: The only difficult thing about this exercise was reminding my patient to take her meds

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a BID patient
Community health worker for a TID patient
chwBarriers: Didn't want to bother my patient
chwStrategies: Discussion of strategies to help them remember

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Helped simulate patient experience
answers.difficultAspects: Students should be allowed to enroll themselves rather than enrolled a few days before start to increase likelihood of investment that would potentially be more similar to a patient's

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a TID patient
answers.beneficialAspects: The system in which we logged our outreaches was user-friendly. I think an app version of it could be even better.
answers.otherComments: I scheduled emails to be sent out at each time the patient was to take the medication. I also cc'ed me to remind me to log that I reminded the patient. Some of my log times (especially early morning) were not indicative of the exact time the reminder was sent to the patient, as I logged the notification later than the patient would have received the email.
chwBarriers: Didn't want to bother my patient
answers.chwBarriersOther: Used scheduled emails to remind patient, so all emails were scheduled well ahead of time to avoid some of these barriers on my end.

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a BID patient
answers.beneficialAspects: I think this exercise would be especially beneficial for students who themselves have never had to take any medications regularly, and thus may not have a good idea of how easy it is to not adhere for a variety of potential reasons. As someone who has taken medications on a regular basis before/helped family members take their own medications, I don't feel like this simulation taught me anything new, since I already understood what it is like to be in the patient's shoes. I see the merit of this simulation and do think it is a testament to how much of a difference a CHW could make in helping a patient stick to the plan. Extra layers of accountability are always helpful. For my patient in this simulation, I had her just text me a check mark emoji with each dose, and I'd text her a reminder if I hadn't received the emoji by a certain time (had an alarm set on my phone to remind myself).
answers.difficultAspects: I didn't have any issues as my patient was pretty on top of everything! I can imagine it would've been more difficult with a patient who wasn't as willing to engage with me or text me after each dose.
chwBarriers: Didn't want to bother my patient
chwStrategies: Discussion of strategies to help them remember

stressors: Changing to a new job or new school

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient with an assigned community health worker
TID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: physical pill bottle
answers.difficultAspects: clarify what the CHW role should be doing, also was not a huge fan of having to log it on the website and use the pill bottle both
answers.pillBottleFeedback: my pill bottle was hard to open/close and at times a part of it would come apart, I am unsure if I was opening it properly

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a BID patient
answers.beneficialAspects: It could all be done virtually.
answers.difficultAspects: The medadhere website went down for a couple days.

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Could be a good simulation exercise for medication adherence.
answers.difficultAspects: Having a specific time range to take a BID or TID medication is too restrictive in my opinion. If a patient takes their medication later in the day (e.g. 11 AM and then later at night (e.g. say 12 AM), I don't think that's much different in compliance compared to 9AM and 9PM provided that you take it twice a day and adequately spaced apart. Also doesn't take into account those that may be traveling and in different time zones at this time. Also had issues with the sensor/cap despite following the recommendations with opening.

answers.assignedRole: Community health worker for a TID patient
chwBarriers: Didn't want to bother my patient
answers.chwBarriersOther: There were original issues with the patient being unsure if she was participating or not so a lot of back and forth communication occurred and we missed a day at the beginning

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Yes, helpful to show how different med adherence expectations are compared to reality. Had a unexpected travel too during the trial, which also highlighted unexpected difficulties.
answers.difficultAspects: Missed a few medadhere logs due to extra steps required. Will be discrepancy between pill bottle data and log data.
answers.pillBottleFeedback_noChw: No issues. Tracking with just pill bottle would be easier then having to log in addition to taking the med.

answers.assignedRole: BID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: The physical act of taking pills.
answers.difficultAspects: Tracking it especially since the website went down.

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: CHW being students
answers.difficultAspects: MedAdhere app had glitches

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient with an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: When the med adhere website was working well, it was great.
answers.difficultAspects: The website breaking down. Also having to carry the pill bottle was slightly unrealistic. For example, i went bowling for a birthday party and instead of bringing a pill in a ziploc bag, i had to bring the entire pill bottle so it was logged correctly.

answers.assignedRole: TID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
TID Patient withOUT an assigned community health worker
answers.beneficialAspects: Logging online and doing pill bottle
answers.difficultAspects: The specific time constraints of 3 times a day - should not have to be in specific time ranges
answers.otherComments: Was not able to log a few days due to error on website
